behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local parsing/segmentation logic for a single command string. Behavior text is about statement splitting only, not cross-module handoff, filesystem, subprocess, or model output. Higher levels rejected: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement and no AI/ML dependency in this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Call the segmenter with a multi-line shell input (e.g., two variable assignments plus a command) and assert it returns distinct ordered segments rather than one block.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at module boundary: segmenter output must feed trigger classifier correctly. Unit-only tests of classifier with pre-segmented input could miss this incident. Integration brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess needed by behavior text, (b) no AI/ML output dependency, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM stage handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Run hook classification path in-process on raw multi-line command text containing newline-separated assignments then `gh pr create`; assert final classification is PR-create trigger (not `not_a_trigger`).

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: Needs same segmentation→classification wiring as behavior 2, but validates negative trigger specificity across modules. Integration brake: no real subprocess/network/OS primitive required by behavior text; no model-output dependence; no multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the same in-process hook parsing/classification flow with a multi-line block ending in `gh issue create`; assert classification is not PR-create trigger.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly combines detection logic with persistence contract (reconstruct + persist identifier), so multiple local modules plus filesystem interaction are required. System rejected because behavior does not require real `gh` subprocess execution or network PR lookup; an in-process fixture plus temp filesystem can catch this boundary failure. No AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: With a detected PR-create event fixture, run hook state-building code end-to-end in-process and assert the persisted state file contains the reconstructed PR identifier in expected fields.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: This is a producer/consumer contract across two hooks via filesystem state, so minimum boundary is cross-module integration. System rejected: behavior text requires read/enforce compatibility, not real OS eventing/subprocess/network. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no model decision steps involved.
behaviors.test_description: In one test flow, run PostToolUse logic to write gate state, then invoke PreToolUse enforcement reader against that file and assert enforcement activates as expected (e.g., gate recognized/blocked).
